headline: Energy Transition Today - Genie Energy: Navigating Financial Reporting Challenges Amidst Transition
summary: Genie Energy Ltd., a provider of retail and renewable energy solutions, announced that it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange regarding a delay in filing its annual report. The delay is due to the necessity of preparing restated audited financial statements for previous years, which would be included in the comprehensive report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025. This development highlights challenges in financial reporting within the energy sector during the ongoing...

headline: Chevron Confirms Oil Discovery at Bandit Prospect in Gulf of America
summary: HOUSTON, April 09, 2026--Chevron Corporation (NYSE:CVX) today confirmed an oil discovery at the Bandit prospect in the Gulf of America, as announced by operator Occidental. The exploration well is located in Green Canyon Block 680, about 125 miles south of the Louisiana coast.

content.title: Chevron Confirms Oil Discovery at Bandit Prospect in Gulf of America
content.summary: HOUSTON, April 09, 2026--Chevron Corporation (NYSE:CVX) today confirmed an oil discovery at the Bandit prospect in the Gulf of America, as announced by operator Occidental. The exploration well is located in Green Canyon Block 680, about 125 miles south of the Louisiana coast.

headline: Chevron Price Prediction: Where Will The Oil Stock Be In 3 Years?
summary: Oil giant Chevron (NYSE:CVX) trades at $197.41 as of writing. Our price target is $202.62, implying 5.13% upside over the next 12 months. headline: Chevron vs. Exxon: Who's benefited the most from higher oil prices?
summary: Truist analysts upgraded the energy sector (XLE) to Attractive from Neutral earlier this week, as oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) climb back to $100 per barrel on Thursday. Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley and Truist CIO Keith Lerner compare the performance of energy giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) as oil prices remain elevated.

content.title: Chevron vs. Exxon: Who's benefited the most from higher oil prices?
content.description:
Truist analysts upgraded the energy sector (XLE) to Attractive from Neutral earlier this week, as oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) climb back to $100 per barrel on Thursday.

Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley and Truist CIO Keith Lerner compare the performance of energy giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) as oil prices remain elevated.

headline: Oil Rises in Choppy Session as Saudi Supplies Hit By Attacks
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Oil climbed in choppy trading as a tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran failed to allay fears of further disruptions to Middle Eastern flows, while shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains largely blocked. West Texas Intermediate’s May contract swung in a more than $7 range before settling higher near $98 a barrel. Brent for June edged up to end the day just below $96. Both benchmarks lifted by roughly $2 in post-settlement trading.Prices rallied into market close as Sau
